ops.text: rm-guard security fix VERIFIED + PUSHED to captain-hook main (c152fbc6, clean FF over 4e1ed257). codex security-verify pass (gpt-5.6-sol via codex-wrapper, codex recovered from usage limit): all 4 changes CONFIRMED fail-closed, empirically tested against real trash/repo/scratch fixtures + all 22 inline rows + 6 ad-hoc probes — no fail-open, no new over-blocks vs the pre-refactor reference. Confirmed the standout: `rm $(ls)`/`rm \`ls\`` was the real fail-open (substitution word lifted → empty targets → fell through to ALLOW), now double-gated (check_call targets.complete + Call.sub substituted-refusal). Two scoped-out nuances: backslash-newline leaf-name is_repo_root edge (narrow; task #17 tightens via cmd.py Call.spliceable), and unverified `$FOO` allow-in-repo-cwd (pre-existing, byte-identical to known-good 0c9f5d9e reference). captain-hook main red is now fixed forward; CI queued behind the ongoing GitHub Actions partial outage (~100min; Git Operations operational so all pushes land — recovery monitor byfe18omu armed to sweep all deferred CI watches when Actions returns). NOTE this means captain-hook main's pre-existing baseline is now GREEN-pending-CI (was red from b65dfe88); a future tag is unblocked once CI confirms. All Phase 1/2/3 trees landed; only CI confirmations + cc-skills closer (fragments/templates deleted per tasks 13/14, prose+version-bump+ship in flight tasks 15/16) remain before Phase 4.
